front: Ele quis aparecer na reunião falando difícil, mas acabou pagando mico.
pronunciation: EH-lee keez ah-pah-reh-SEHR nah rrehoo-nee-ONG fah-LAHN-doh jee-FEE-seeo, mahz ah-kah-BOH pah-GAHN-doh MEE-koo.
back: He wanted to show off in the meeting by using big words, but ended up making a fool of himself.
context: Using 'pagar mico' (to pay a little monkey) to mean embarrassing oneself or committing a social gaffe.

front: Depois daquela fofoca maldosa, a reputação dela ficou com o filme queimado.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z dah-KEH-lyah foh-FOH-kah mahl-DOH-zah, ah rreh-poo-tah-ZOHNG DEH-lyah fee-KOH kohm oh FEEL-mee kay-MAH-doh.
back: After that nasty gossip, her reputation was completely ruined.
context: Using 'ficar com o filme queimado' (to have one's film burned) as the passive state of having a damaged reputation.

front: Não adianta ficar batendo na tecla de que a culpa foi dele; o problema já passou.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah fee-KAHR bah-TEHN-do nah TEH-klah jee keh ah KOOL-pah FOY DEH-lee; oh proh-BLEH-mah zha_pah-SOH.
back: It's no use harping on the fact that it was his fault; the problem is already in the past.
context: Using 'bater na mesma tecla' (to keep hitting the same key) to mean repeating the same point or argument over and over.

front: Fiquei sabendo que eles vão se casar por meio da rádio peão da fábrica.
pronunciation: fee-KAY sah-BEHN-doh keh EH-leez vONG see kah-ZAHR pohr MAY-oo dah RRAH-djoo pyONG dah FAH-bree-kah.
back: I found out they are getting married through the company grapevine.
context: Using 'rádio peão' (laborer's radio) to refer to the workplace grapevine or informal rumor mill.

front: Ela tentou me passar para trás na divisão da herança, mas eu percebi a tempo.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH mee pah-SAHR PAH-rah TRAHZ nah jee-vee-ZOHNG dah eh-RAn-sah, mahz ew pehr-seh-BEE ah TEHM-poo.
back: She tried to cheat me in the division of the inheritance, but I noticed in time.
context: Using 'passar para trás' to mean cheating, swindling, or double-crossing someone.

front: O projeto estava indo super bem, mas o novo gerente resolveu mudar tudo e botou o carro na frente dos bois.
pronunciation: oh proh-ZHEH-toh ees-TAH-vah EENG-doh SOO-pehr BEHNG, mahz oh NOH-voo zheh-REHN-chee rreh-sohl-VEW moo-DAHR TOO-doh ee boh-TOH oh KAHR-rroo nah FREHN-chee doohz BOY_Z.
back: The project was going really well, but the new manager decided to change everything and put the cart before the horse.
context: An alternative verb usage ('botar' instead of 'pôr') for the classic idiom of rushing processes out of order.

front: Quando soube que a viagem foi cancelada, ele rodou a baiana na agência de turismo.
pronunciation: KWAHN-doh SOH-bee keh ah VYAH-zheng FOY kahn-seh-LAH-dah, EH-lee rroh-DOH ah bah-YAH-nah nah ah-ZHEHN-syah jee too-REES-moo.
back: When he found out the trip was canceled, he threw a massive fit at the travel agency.
context: Using 'rodar a baiana' (to spin the Bahian woman) to mean making a scene, throwing a fit, or confronting someone fiercely.

front: Não aguento mais esse vizinho barulhento; hoje vou lá chutar o pau da barraca.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-GWEHN-too MYS EHS-see vee-ZEENG-yoo bah-roo-LYEHN-too; HOY-zhee voy LAH shoo-TAHR oh PAW dah bahr-RAH-kah.
back: I can't stand this noisy neighbor anymore; today I'm going over there to kick up a massive row.
context: Using 'chutar o pau da barraca' (to kick the tent pole) to mean blowing one's top, causing a major scene, or abandoning all civility.

front: Ele adora tirar uma casquinha do sucesso dos outros sem fazer esforço algum.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah tee-RAHR OO-mah kahs-KEENG-yah doh soo-SEHS-soo doohz OW-troohz sehng fah-ZEHR ees-FOHR-soo ah_GOONG.
back: He loves to latch onto other people's success and get a free ride without making any effort.
context: Using 'tirar uma casquinha' (to take a little rind/shaving) to mean taking advantage of a situation, flirting subtly, or riding on someone's coattails.

front: O debate político na televisão acabou virando um verdadeiro balaio de gatos.
pronunciation: oh deh-BAH-chee poh-LEE-chee nah cheh-leh-vee-ZOHNG ah-kah-BOH vee-RAHN-doh oong vehr-dah-DAY-roo bah-LAY-oo jee GAH-toohz.
back: The political debate on television ended up turning into a complete chaotic mess.
context: Using 'balaio de gatos' (a basket of cats) to describe an extremely messy, confusing, or noisy situation.

front: Ela tentou me enrolar com uma conversa mole, mas eu sei bem como as coisas funcionam.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH mee ehn-rro-LAHR kohm OO-mah kohn-VEHR-sah MOH-lee, mahz ew SAY BEHNG KOH-moo as KOY-zahz foon-syo-NAHG.
back: She tried to stall me with smooth talk, but I know very well how things work.
context: Using 'conversa mole' (soft talk) to mean sweet-talk, excuses, or BS meant to deceive or delay.

front: Se você não se planejar financeiramente, vai acabar vendendo o almoço para comprar o jantar.
pronunciation: see voh-SEH NOHNG see plah-neh-ZHAHR fee-nahn-say-rah-MEHN-chee, VAY ah-kah-BAHR vehn-DEHN-doh oh ahl-MOH-soo PAH-rah kohm-PRAHR oh zhan-TAHR.
back: If you don't plan financially, you will end up living hand to mouth.
context: Using 'vender o almoço para comprar o jantar' (to sell lunch to buy dinner) to describe living in extreme financial precarity.

front: Acho melhor você maneirar no tom para não procurar sarna para se coçar.
pronunciation: AH-shoo meh-LYOHR voh-SEH mah-nay-RAHR noo TOHNG PAH-rah NOHNG proh-koo-RAHR SAHR-nah PAH-rah see koh-SAHR.
back: I think you'd better tone it down so you don't look for trouble.
context: Using 'procurar sarna para se coçar' (to look for scabies to scratch) to mean unnecessarily inviting trouble or complications.

front: Ela está com dor de cotovelo desde que viu o ex-namorado com outra pessoa.
pronunciation: EH-lyah ees-TAH kohm DOHR jee koh-toh-VEH-loo DEHS-jee keh VEE_oo oh EX-nah-moh-RAH-doh kohm OW-trah peh-SOH-ah.
back: She has been feeling jealous ever since she saw her ex-boyfriend with someone else.
context: Using 'dor de cotovelo' (elbow pain) to describe jealousy, heartache, or pining over an ex-partner.

front: O diretor de arte gosta de inventar moda e sempre traz ideias impraticáveis para o estúdio.
pronunciation: oh jee-reh-TOH_r jee AHR-chee GOHS-tah jee eeng-vehn-TAHR MOH-dah ee SEHM-pree TRAH_Z ee-DAY-ahz eeng-prah-chee-KAH-veeez PAH-rah oh ees-TOO-djoo.
back: The art director loves to overcomplicate things and always brings impractical ideas to the studio.
context: Using 'inventar moda' to mean complicating a simple process, innovating unnecessarily, or trying weird new things.

front: Ele é um tremendo mala sem alça; ninguém aguenta cinco minutos de conversa com ele.
pronunciation: EH-lee eh oong treh-MEHN-doo MAH-lyah sehng AHL-sah; neheng-GEHNG ah-GWEHN-tah SEENG-koo mee-NOO-toohz jee kohn-VEHR-sah kohm EH-lee.
back: He is an absolute pain in the neck; nobody can stand five minutes of conversation with him.
context: Using 'mala sem alça' (a suitcase without a handle) to describe an annoying, boring, or tedious person who is hard to get rid of.

front: A noiva estava linda, mas o atraso de duas horas foi de amargar.
pronunciation: ah NOY-vah ees-TAH-vah LEEN-dah, mahz oh ah-TRAH-zoo jee DOO-ahz OH-rahz FOY jee ah-mahr-GAHR.
back: The bride looked beautiful, but the two-hour delay was hard to swallow.
context: Using 'de amargar' (bitter/to make bitter) to describe something highly unpleasant, disappointing, or tough to tolerate.

front: Não adianta vir com chororô agora; você teve todas as chances de entregar o projeto no prazo.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah VEER kohm sho-roo-ROH ah-GOH-rah; voh-SEH CHEH-vee TOH-dahz az SHAn-seez jee een-treh-GAHR oh proh-ZHEH-toh noo PRAH-zoo.
back: There's no point in whining now; you had every chance to deliver the project on time.
context: Using 'chororô' colloquially to describe excessive, childish complaining, whining, or crying.

front: Sinto que estou dando murro em ponta de faca tentando explicar lógica de programação para ele.
pronunciation: SEENG-too keh ees-TOY DAHN-doh MOO-rroo ehng POHN-tah jee FAH-kah tehn-TAHN-doh ees-plee-KAHR LOH-zhee-kah jee proh-grah-mah-SOHNG PAH-rah EH-lee.
back: I feel like I'm beating my head against a brick wall trying to explain programming logic to him.
context: Another application of 'dar murro em ponta de faca', emphasizing a frustrating, unproductive effort.

front: Não dê corda para ele, senão ele vai passar a tarde inteira falando sobre política.
pronunciation: NOHNG DEH KOHR-dah PAH-rah EH-lee, see-NONG EH-lee VAY pah-SAHR ah TAHR-jee een-TAY-rah fah-LAHN-doh SOH-bree poh-LEE-chee.
back: Don't encourage him, otherwise he will spend the whole afternoon talking about politics.
context: Using 'dar corda' (to give rope) to mean encouraging someone to talk or act, often leading to excess.

front: Depois de ser pego mentindo, ele ficou com uma cara de tacho impagável.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee SEHR PEH-goo meeng-CHEENG-doh, EH-lee fee-KOH kohm OO-mah KAH-rah jee TAH-shoo eeng-pah-GAH-vehl.
back: After being caught lying, he had a priceless look of embarrassment on his face.
context: Using 'ficar com cara de tacho' (to have a face like a pan) to describe looking sheepish, embarrassed, or silly after being caught or disappointed.

front: O show de rock foi o bicho; a energia do público estava simplesmente incrível.
pronunciation: oh SHOW jee RROHK FOY oh BEE-shoo; ah eh-nehr-ZHEE-ah doh POO-blee-koh ees-TAH-vah seem-pleez-MEHN-chee eeng-KREE-vehl.
back: The rock concert was awesome; the energy of the crowd was simply amazing.
context: Using 'ser o bicho' (to be the beast/animal) to mean being awesome, highly impressive, or excellent.

front: Ela tentou tapar o sol com a peneira fingindo que o casamento ia bem para as amigas.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH tah-PAHR oh SOHL kohm ah peh-NAY-rah feeng-ZHEHN-doh keh oh kah-zah-MEHN-toh EE-ah BEHNG PAH-rah az ah-MEE-gahz.
back: She tried to cover up the cracks by pretending to her friends that her marriage was going well.
context: Another relational context for the classic 'tapar o sol com a peneira'.

front: Não gosto de fazer negócios com quem quer sempre levar vantagem em tudo.
pronunciation: NOHNG GOHS-too jee fah-ZEHR neh-GOH-syoohz kohm KEHNG KEHR SEHM-pree leh-VAHR vahn-TAH-zheng ehng TOO-doh.
back: I don't like doing business with people who always want to take advantage of everything.
context: Using 'levar vantagem' to mean gaining an upper hand, sometimes unscrupulously (referencing 'Lei de Gerson').

front: Se eu fosse você, não contaria com aquele dinheiro; é melhor colocar as barbas de molho.
pronunciation: see_oo FOHS-see voh-SEH, NOHNG kohn-tah-REE-ah kohm ah-KEH-lee jee-NYAY-roo; eh meh-LYOHR koh-loh-KAHR ahz BAHR-bahz jee MOH-lyoo.
back: If I were you, I wouldn't count on that money; it's better to play it safe.
context: Using 'colocar as barbas de molho' to advise being highly cautious and defensive.

front: Ele acabou entregando o ouro ao revelar a estratégia secreta da empresa sem querer.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-kah-BOH een-treh-GAHN-doh oh OW-roo_oo rreh-veh-LAHR ah ees-trah-TEH-zhyah seh-GREH-tah dah ehm-PREH-zah sehng keh-REHR.
back: He ended up giving the game away by accidentally revealing the company's secret strategy.
context: Using 'entregar o ouro' (to hand over the gold) to mean giving away a secret, advantage, or letting down a defense.

front: Ela é muito tímida, mas quando bebe um pouco, acaba soltando a franga na pista.
pronunciation: EH-lyah eh MWEE-too JEE-mee-dah, mahz KWAHN-doh BEH-bee oong POH-koo, ah-KAH-bah sohl-TAHN-doh ah FRAHN-gah nah PEES-tah.
back: She is very shy, but when she drinks a little, she ends up letting her hair down on the dance floor.
context: A social context for 'soltar a franga' meaning to abandon shyness or reserve.

front: O trânsito de São Paulo é de enlouquecer; às vezes dá vontade de chutar o balde.
pronunciation: oh TRAN-zee-too jee SOHNG PAW-loo eh jee eheng-low-keh-SEHR; ahz VEH-zeez DAH vohn-TAH-jee jee shoo-TAHR oh BAHL-jee.
back: The traffic in São Paulo is maddening; sometimes you just feel like giving up and letting go.
context: Using 'chutar o balde' to represent a sudden loss of patience or abandon of effort.

front: Eles prometeram que entregariam o projeto na segunda, mas nos deixaram na mão.
pronunciation: EH-leez proh-meh-TEH-roong keh een-treh-gah-REE-ahong oh proh-ZHEH-toh nah seh-GOON-dah, mahz noohz day-SHAH-roong nah MONG.
back: They promised they would deliver the project on Monday, but they left us in the lurch.
context: Using 'deixar na mão' to express being abandoned, disappointed, or unsupported by someone when needed.

front: Eu ia viajar no feriado, mas meus planos foram para o vinagre devido ao trabalho extra.
pronunciation: ew EE-ah vyah-ZHAHR noo feh-ree-AH-doh, mahz MEW_Z PLAH-noohz FOH-roong PAH-rah oh vee-NAH-gree deh-VEE-doh AH-oo trah-BAH-lyoo EX-trah.
back: I was going to travel on the holiday, but my plans went down the drain due to extra work.
context: Using 'ir para o vinagre' to describe personal or professional plans failing completely.

front: O preço daquela passagem de última hora estava salgado, mas tive que comprar.
pronunciation: oh PREH-soo dah-KEH-lyah pah-SAH-zheng jee OOL-chee-mah OH-rah ees-TAH-vah sahl-GAH-doh, mahz CHEE-vee keh kohm-PRAHR.
back: The price of that last-minute ticket was steep, but I had to buy it.
context: Using 'salgado' (salty) colloquially to mean expensive or overpriced.

front: Não adianta tentar fazer média com o chefe; ele prefere ver resultados concretos.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR fah-ZEHR MEH-dyah kohm oh SHEH-fee; EH-lee preh-FEE-ree VEHR rreh-zool-TAH-doohz kohn-KREH-toohz.
back: There's no use trying to curry favor with the boss; he prefers to see concrete results.
context: Using 'fazer média' to mean trying to please others, curry favor, or make a good impression artificially.

front: A ideia do piquenique na praia foi por água abaixo quando começou a trovejar.
pronunciation: ah ee-DAY-ah doh pee-kee-NEE-kee nah PRY-ah FOY pohr AH-gwah ah-BAY-shoo KWAHN-doh koh-meh-SOH ah troh-veh-ZHAHR.
back: The idea of a picnic on the beach fell through when it started to thunder.
context: Using 'ir por água abaixo' to describe plans getting completely ruined by external factors.

front: Fiquei com a pulga atrás da orelha quando o preço do produto caiu pela metade sem explicação.
pronunciation: fee-KAY kohm ah POOL-gah ah-TRAHZ dah oh-REHL-yah KWAHN-doh oh PREH-soo doh proh-DOO-too kah-EE PEH-lah meh-TAH-jee sehng ees-plee-kah-SOHNG.
back: I became highly suspicious when the price of the product dropped by half without explanation.
context: Using 'com a pulga atrás da orelha' to express intuitive doubt or wariness about an offer.

front: Não gosto de sair com quem fica contando vantagem sobre carros caros o tempo todo.
pronunciation: NOHNG GOHS-too jee sah-EER kohm KEHNG FEE-kah kohn-TAHN-doh vahn-TAH-zheng SOH-bree KAHR-rroohz KAH-roohz oh TEHM-poo TOH-doh.
back: I don't like going out with people who keep bragging about expensive cars all the time.
context: Using 'contar vantagem' in a social context to mean bragging or boasting.

front: Depois de tanto esforço, nossa equipe conseguiu dar a volta por cima e vencer o torneio.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-too ees-FOHR-soo, NOHS-sah ee-KEE-pee kohn-seh-GHEW dahr ah VOHL-tah pohr SEE-mah ee vehn-SEHR oh tohr-NAY-oo.
back: After so much effort, our team managed to turn things around and win the tournament.
context: A sporting context for 'dar a volta por cima' emphasizing a triumphant comeback.

front: Ele adora meter o bedelho na organização da festa, mesmo sem ter sido convidado a ajudar.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah meh-TEHR oh beh-DEH-lyoo nah ohr-gah-nee-zah-SOHNG dah FES-tah, MEHS-moo sehng TEHR SEE-doh kohn-vee-DAH-do_ah ah-zhoo-DAHR.
back: He loves to stick his nose into the party planning, even without having been asked to help.
context: Using 'meter o bedelho' to describe unwelcome interference or meddling.

front: Aquela prova de física foi de cair o queixo de tão difícil que estava.
pronunciation: ah-KEH-lyah PROH-vah jee FEE-zee-kah FOY jee kah-EER oh KAY-shoo jee TOHNG jee-FEE-seeo keh ees-TAH-vah.
back: That physics exam was absolutely mind-bogglingly difficult.
context: Using 'de cair o queixo' metaphorically to express shock at extreme difficulty.

front: Fique atento para não comer mosca e perder a data de inscrição no curso.
pronunciation: FEE-kee ah-TEHN-too PAH-rah NOHNG koh-MEHR MOHS-kah ee pehr-DEHR ah DAH-tah jee eengs-kree-SOHNG noo KOOR-soo.
back: Stay alert so you don't miss the deadline and lose the course registration date.
context: Using 'comer mosca' to describe missing a critical detail due to carelessness or distraction.

front: O plano de marketing deles parecia bom, mas acabou caindo do cavalo na hora de executar.
pronunciation: oh PLAH-noo jee MAHR-keh-teeng DEH-leez pah-reh-SEE-ah BOHNG, mahz ah-kah-BOH kah-EENG-doh doh kah-VAH-loo nah OH-rah jee ee-zeh-koo-TAHR.
back: Their marketing plan seemed good, but it fell flat on its face when it came to execution.
context: Using 'cair do cavalo' to describe experiencing a sudden, disappointing failure after high confidence.

front: Precisamos separar o joio do trigo para escolher os melhores currículos para a vaga.
pronunciation: pree-see-ZAH-mohz seh-pah-RAHR oh ZHOY-oo doh TREE-goo PAH-rah ees-koh-LYEHR ooz meh-LYOH-reez koohr-REE-koo-loohz PAH-rah ah VAH-gah.
back: We need to separate the wheat from the chaff to choose the best resumes for the position.
context: Using 'separar o joio do trigo' in a professional recruitment context.

front: Ela tentou dar uma de João sem braço quando perguntei quem quebrou o vaso.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH dahr OO-mah jee zhoh-ONG sehng BRAH-soo KWAHN-doh pehr-goon-TAY KEHNG kweh-BROH oh VAH-zoo.
back: She tried to play dumb when I asked who broke the vase.
context: Using 'dar uma de João sem braço' to describe someone pretending to know nothing about a mistake.

front: O novo assistente é uma mão na roda para organizar a agenda diária.
pronunciation: oh NOH-voo ah-sees-TEHN-chee eh OO-mah MONG nah RROH-dah PAH-rah ohr-gah-nee-ZAHR ah ah-ZHHEHN-dah jee-AH-ryah.
back: The new assistant is a lifesaver for organizing the daily schedule.
context: Using 'uma mão na roda' to describe a highly useful tool or helpful person.

front: Não compre esse equipamento sem testar antes para não comprar gato por lebre.
pronunciation: NOHNG KOHM-pree EHS-see ee-kee-pah-MEHN-too sehng chehs-TAHR AHN-teez PAH-rah NOHNG kohm-PRAHR GAH-too pohr LEH-bree.
back: Don't buy this equipment without testing it first so you don't get ripped off.
context: Using 'comprar gato por lebre' to warn against deceptive trades or faulty products.

front: Ele passou a tarde inteira chorando as pitangas por causa do pneu furado.
pronunciation: EH-lee pah-SOH_ah TAHR-jee een-TAY-rah shoh-RAHN-doh ahz pee-TAHN-gahz pohr KOW-zah doh pneh-oo foo-RAH-doh.
back: He spent the entire afternoon whining about the flat tire.
context: Using 'chorar as pitangas' to describe exaggerated whining over minor setbacks.
